text: My doctor prescribed Humira for my rheumatoid arthritis and I've been waiting 3 weeks for the prior authorization to go through. My joints are getting worse while insurance plays games.
The prior auth process for my insulin pump supplies is a nightmare. I have to resubmit every 6 months even though I've been a diabetic for 20 years. Nothing has changed.
Specialty pharmacy keeps saying they're waiting on my insurance to approve my multiple sclerosis medication. Meanwhile I'm 2 weeks past my last dose. This is dangerous.

I was denied coverage for my migraine medication because insurance said I have to try 3 other medications first. I already tried them years ago and they didn't work. Now I have to do it all over again.
Called the pharmacy line 4 times this week. Each person tells me something different about why my prior auth was denied. No one can give me a straight answer.
My antidepressant was moved to a higher tier on the formulary. My monthly cost went from $45 to $210 overnight. I can't afford that on my current budget.
The prior authorization for my ADHD medication takes 2 weeks every single time I need a refill. It's the same medication I've been on for 5 years. Why do I have to keep proving I need it?
Denied my Ozempic prescription for diabetes management. My A1C is dangerously high and my doctor says this is the right treatment but insurance apparently knows better.
Specialty pharmacy called to tell me my biologics would be $4,200 after insurance. That can't be right. No one can explain how they arrived at that number.
Step therapy is ridiculous. My doctor said methotrexate is wrong for my condition but I have to fail on it first before insurance will cover what actually works.
Prior auth submitted 3 times for my sleep apnea medication. Three times denied. My doctor keeps adding documentation and they keep finding new reasons to deny it.
The formulary they sent me at the start of the year was already out of date. Found out at the pharmacy when my blood pressure medication wasn't covered anymore.
Trying to use mail order pharmacy to save money but the transfer has been stuck for 6 weeks. Still paying full retail prices at the local pharmacy in the meantime.
My 6 year old's EpiPen prior auth was denied. An EpiPen. For a child with a documented severe peanut allergy. How is this even possible.
Got a letter saying my exception request for a non-formulary drug was denied. Letter came 3 days after the deadline to appeal. Suspicious timing.
Copay for my brand name medication is $75 but the generic isn't available yet. Called to ask about a copay assistance program and was told I don't qualify because I have insurance.
Specialty pharmacy shipped my medication to my old address even though I updated it online twice. Now I have to wait another 2 weeks for a replacement shipment approval.
Prior auth for my CPAP machine was denied because they said the sleep study wasn't recent enough. I had it done 13 months ago and their policy says 12 months. For a machine I already use every night.
Tried to fill a 90-day supply of my maintenance medication but the system only allowed 30 days even though my plan covers 90-day fills. Took 4 calls to correct.
My chemotherapy medication requires a new prior authorization every 6 weeks. I'm fighting cancer and spending energy on insurance paperwork instead of healing.
The prior auth form my doctor submitted was rejected because it wasn't on the right form version. No one told us there was a specific required form until after the deadline passed.
Been trying to get my antifungal prescription approved for 3 weeks. The infection is getting worse while we wait for a decision.
Medication denied under the formulary exception process. The denial letter said I could appeal but the phone number on the letter is disconnected.
Humira is on my plan's formulary but the specialty pharmacy says my plan requires me to use their pharmacy exclusively. Found this out after 2 months of trying to fill it elsewhere.
My anxiety medication was covered last year but isn't this year. Nobody notified me the formulary changed until I tried to fill it in January.
Prior auth approved then reversed 3 days later with no explanation. My medication is sitting at the pharmacy and I cannot pick it up.
The specialty pharmacy my insurance requires doesn't deliver and is 45 minutes away. My previous pharmacy was 5 minutes from my house and offered delivery.
I need a medication that isn't on the formulary but is the only FDA-approved option for my condition. Exception request denied. What exactly are my options?
My prescription drug plan changed this year and now my heart medication costs $340 a month instead of $85. I'm on a fixed income and may have to stop taking it.
Prior auth for my biologics is expiring and the renewal form was sent to the wrong doctor. We've been trying to fix this for 5 weeks with no resolution.
The pharmacist told me my insurance rejected the prescription because it requires prior auth. No one from my insurance ever informed me this drug required authorization.
I had to call my insurance 8 times to get the specialty pharmacy to actually ship my medication. Every call I was told it would ship within 24 hours.
My asthma inhaler moved from tier 2 to tier 3. It now costs $115 instead of $35. My inhaler. The one I need to breathe.
Prior auth denied because my doctor didn't include the right diagnosis code. This is a coding issue on the submission not a medical decision. Still took 3 weeks to fix.
Mail order pharmacy lost my refill order. They have no record of it even though I have a confirmation number. Starting a new order means another 2-week wait.
My son's growth hormone medication requires a prior authorization AND a separate exception request every year. The process takes 6 weeks and he cannot miss doses.
I asked for a prior auth exception for a medication my previous insurance covered for 4 years. Denied because my new plan considers it experimental.
Step therapy requirement says I have to try and fail two cheaper drugs before they'll cover the one that works for my neuropathic pain. My doctor has documented I've already failed those drugs.
My eye drops for glaucoma aren't covered. The alternative they suggest isn't approved for my specific type of glaucoma. My doctor says the alternative isn't safe for my case.
Getting a prior authorization took longer than the infection itself. My antibiotic was finally approved 4 days after I finished the sample pack my doctor gave me.
I enrolled in a new plan and my current medications weren't on the formulary. Found out at the pharmacy counter. My enrollment agent never checked drug coverage.
Prior auth approved for 30 days only. My prescription is for a 90-day supply. Why authorize one month at a time for a chronic lifelong condition?
I switched plans during open enrollment and nobody told me my specialty medication would require prior auth under the new plan. Now I have a 3-week gap in medication.
The copay tier for my blood thinner changed mid-year without any notice. My out of pocket went from $30 to $95 per fill with no warning.
Prior auth for post-surgical pain medication denied because it's an opioid. I just had spinal surgery. What are patients supposed to do for pain management?
Formulary exception was approved but on the condition I use the mail order pharmacy. Mail order said the prior auth number from the exception doesn't match what they need.
My insulin was moved to a non-preferred tier. The copay difference is $185 a month. Insulin. The medication I need to survive every single day.
Specialty medication approved by prior auth but the specialty pharmacy my insurance requires doesn't have it in stock and has no estimate of when they will.
Prior auth shows as approved in my insurance portal but was rejected at the pharmacy. Spent 2 hours on hold trying to get someone to reconcile why.
My pharmacy told me the prior auth was approved but I still had to pay full price because the approval hadn't been transmitted to the pharmacy yet. Now I need to get reimbursed somehow.
Submitted a claim for my son's ER visit in October. It's now March and the claim is still showing as in process. Five calls later and still no resolution.
Received an explanation of benefits showing I owe $2,400 for a procedure my doctor said was fully covered. Nobody can explain why the claim was denied.
The hospital billed the wrong insurance code and my claim was denied. I shouldn't have to be the one to fix a billing error that I didn't make.
I've been getting balance billed by an out-of-network anesthesiologist even though my surgery was at an in-network hospital. I didn't choose my anesthesiologist.
My claim was processed and then reprocessed twice and each time the amount I owe is different. I don't know which bill to pay or what the correct amount actually is.
Spent 3 hours on the phone trying to understand my EOB. The codes and explanations are completely incomprehensible to any normal person.
Filed a claim appeal 4 months ago. Still no decision. Health insurance is legally required to respond within 60 days and they have ignored that completely.
Claim denied because the diagnosis code and procedure code allegedly don't match. My doctor coded it exactly as the procedure was performed. This is a claims processing error on their end.
The out-of-pocket maximum shown on my portal doesn't match what shows on my EOB. Which number is correct? My deductible situation affects real financial decisions.
I got a bill from my lab for $890 that I thought my insurance covered. My plan says labs are covered at 100% after deductible. My deductible was met back in February.
Two claims for the same visit were processed differently. One was paid and one was denied. Same date, same provider, same type of service with no explanation.
I submitted receipts for reimbursement on 4 procedures. Three were reimbursed and one wasn't. No explanation was given for the inconsistent treatment.
My insurance paid the wrong amount on a claim and now the provider is billing me the difference. Insurance says it was processed correctly. Provider says it wasn't. I'm stuck in the middle.
Claim for my mammogram was denied because they coded it as diagnostic instead of preventive. My doctor's office coded it as preventive. This has been going back and forth for 6 months.
I've hit my out-of-pocket maximum for the year but dental claims are still being processed with member cost share. My plan documents say OOP max covers dental.
My therapist submitted claims that were denied as out-of-network but she is listed as in-network on the insurance website. The directory has been wrong for months.
Claim denied as a duplicate even though the first claim was also denied. So a duplicate of a denied claim is the explanation? None of this makes sense.
I paid a bill that was later covered by insurance but the refund from the provider never arrived. Now both the provider and insurance say to talk to the other one.
My newborn's claims weren't processed because she wasn't added to my policy fast enough after birth. She had a medical emergency at 3 days old. Now I'm being asked to pay $18,000.
Claim for physical therapy was denied because pre-authorization wasn't obtained. My doctor's office says they submitted the authorization. Nobody can locate it.
I've been submitting the same claim for 4 months. Each time I'm told something is missing. I provide what they ask. Then something else is supposedly missing.
Coordination of benefits is completely broken. My primary and secondary insurance have been going back and forth for 8 months and my unpaid bills are going to collections.
EOB shows I owe $540 for my hip replacement but when I called the hospital they say I owe $1,200. Who do I pay when both numbers come from official sources?
My claim was denied as not medically necessary for a procedure my doctor ordered. My doctor submitted supporting documentation twice and it was denied both times.
Reimbursement for out-of-network emergency care has been pending for 5 months. I paid out of pocket during an emergency and cannot recover my money.
My insurance processed my knee surgery claim as outpatient when it was clearly inpatient with an overnight stay. The reimbursement is $1,000 less because of this error.
Claim for my sleep study shows $1,800 in member responsibility. My deductible was met 3 months ago. This should be 80/20 coinsurance not full cost.
I've been charged cost-sharing for a preventive colonoscopy. Preventive screenings are supposed to be covered at 100% under the ACA. I've called three times and nothing changes.
Getting an itemized bill is apparently impossible. The hospital won't provide one and insurance says they only see what the hospital submits. I just need to know what I'm paying for.
My claims for out-of-state emergency care were denied because I didn't call for authorization in advance. It was a genuine emergency. I was unconscious when I arrived.
Claim filed March 2nd. Portal shows processing. Called April 10th and was told still processing. Called May 8th and was told they have no record of the claim at all.
Insurance paid my provider then reversed the payment 6 weeks later with no explanation. Provider is now billing me the full amount and insurance says they'll look into it.
Filed a claim for my daughter's mental health visit. Denied because the therapist's NPI number was entered incorrectly. This same therapist's claims have been paid for 2 years.
My annual deductible resets every calendar year but claims from December are being applied to January's deductible instead. I've been fighting this error for 3 months.
Claim for my husband's cardiac catheterization was denied as not medically necessary. The denial says it wasn't medically necessary. He was having a heart attack when it was performed.
Two different agents told me two completely different things about how my claim was processed. I am getting contradictory information from the same company every time I call.
Charged full price at an urgent care listed as in-network. They say insurance processed it as out-of-network. Insurance says it processed correctly. I owe $400 with no resolution.
I can see my claim in the portal but cannot download or print the EOB. The download button simply does not work. I need this document for my annual taxes.
Claim denied because my physician's office submitted under an incorrect group NPI number. This is an administrative error I had nothing to do with. I shouldn't owe full cost.
Out-of-pocket maximum was met in August. I've been paying copays since September and getting reimbursed requires a written request with a 90-day processing window.
My chiropractor has submitted the same claim 3 times and it keeps being rejected as a duplicate even though the previous submissions were also rejected and never processed.
The claims appeals process requires a written letter sent by certified mail. It is 2024 and this feels like it's deliberately designed to discourage people from appealing.
My prescription drug claim shows a higher cost share than what I actually paid at the pharmacy. Now I'm being asked to pay the difference on top of what I already paid.
Claim for a second opinion was denied as a duplicate of my original consultation even though it was a completely different doctor providing a different professional opinion.
I paid full price for my MRI because the claim wasn't pre-authorized. The pre-authorization was submitted by my doctor and I have the confirmation number. Still denied.
Claims for physical therapy sessions are being rejected one by one with no pattern. Some sessions approved and some denied. My PT can't get a straight answer from anyone either.
I submitted a claim for durable medical equipment 4 months ago. I was told I'd receive a decision in 30 days. I am still waiting with no status updates.
Balance billing from a pathologist at my in-network hospital. The pathologist read my biopsy results and apparently isn't in-network. I didn't select this pathologist.
My claim was partially paid but the denial reason says not medically necessary. If it wasn't medically necessary why was any portion of it paid at all?
EOB was sent to my old address even though I updated it. I missed the appeal window because I never received the denial letter in time to respond.
My primary care doctor retired and I've been trying for 3 months to find a new in-network doctor who is accepting patients. The provider directory is so outdated it's useless.
Referral to a specialist takes 2 weeks to process. Meanwhile my condition is getting worse and there's nothing I can do but wait for a piece of paper to move forward.
The cardiologist my doctor referred me to is listed as in-network on the website. When I called to schedule they told me they dropped the network 6 months ago. The directory is dangerously wrong.
Mental health provider availability in my area is terrible. Every therapist who accepts my insurance has a 3 to 6 month waiting list. I'm in crisis not planning ahead for fun.
My telehealth appointment was scheduled but the doctor never joined the video call. Customer service said they'd reschedule for next week. I took time off work for nothing.
I needed to go to the emergency room and the closest ER is out of network. I was having chest pains. I did not have time to search for an in-network ER during a cardiac emergency.
My hospital for my scheduled surgery was acquired by a health system and is now out-of-network for my plan. I found out 4 days before my surgery date and had to cancel.
The only psychiatrist who accepts my insurance in my county has a 5-month wait for a new patient appointment. I have a bipolar diagnosis that needs active management.
Telehealth visit was denied because the provider wasn't in-network. The telehealth platform is listed on your website as a covered in-network service.
I need to see a neurologist but every neurologist in the provider directory within 30 miles isn't accepting new patients. When does the directory reflect who is actually available?
My daughter has severe anxiety and we were referred to a child psychologist. The only in-network child psychologist in our area doesn't see patients under 16. She's 13 years old.
Had a telehealth appointment and was told to go to the ER for a follow-up. Went to the nearest ER. It was out-of-network. Nobody warned me to check network status during an urgent situation.
I've been trying to find an in-network orthopedic surgeon for 6 weeks. Every office I call is either not accepting patients or not in-network. My knee is deteriorating.
My OB-GYN left the network in the middle of my pregnancy. I'm 7 months pregnant and now have to find a new doctor who will accept me this late in pregnancy.
Referral to physical therapy was denied because I need to get a second opinion from another doctor first. I just want to start PT and recover from my injury.
The provider directory says my gastroenterologist is in-network. She billed as out-of-network. Insurance told me the directory can be delayed in reflecting network changes. That's not acceptable.
Mental health parity is required by law but finding an in-network therapist who actually accepts my insurance is nearly impossible. The ones listed don't have openings for 4 months.
I had to drive 90 minutes each way to find an in-network specialist because everyone closer is out of network. The network in my rural area is extremely thin.
The telehealth platform says my plan is accepted. My insurance says the visit was out-of-network. I'm being billed $340 for a video call I was told was fully covered.
I called my insurance to confirm I don't need a referral for a specialist. Was told I don't. The specialist visit was then denied because no referral was on file.
My plan requires a referral for specialist visits but my PCP is 3 weeks out for a new appointment. I have to wait 3 weeks just to get a referral to start any specialist process.
The urgent care that accepts walk-ins and is 2 miles from my house is out-of-network. The in-network urgent care is 25 miles away and requires an appointment 2 days ahead.
Finding a dentist who accepts my dental plan in my neighborhood is almost impossible. Many in the directory aren't taking new patients or haven't been in-network for years.
I was hospitalized and discharged with a follow-up to a specialist. The specialist my hospital recommended is not in my plan's network. Nobody told me before discharge.
My son has autism and needs behavioral therapy. The one in-network ABA provider in our area has a 14-month waiting list and my insurance won't cover any out-of-network providers.
Telehealth seems like a great option but the app crashes every single time I try to connect with a provider. I've missed multiple appointments and been charged no-show fees.
I asked for a gap exception because there are no in-network providers for my specialty in my area. Denied. They claim there are adequate providers. The closest one is 2.5 hours away.
My in-network PCP referred me to an in-network specialist. The specialist used a hospitalist who turned out to be out-of-network. I had no way of knowing this in advance.
Finding a mental health provider who accepts my insurance and isn't a 6-month wait is basically impossible. I've called more than 20 numbers from the directory with no luck.
I live in a rural area and there are literally 2 in-network providers within a reasonable distance. Both are completely overwhelmed and neither is taking new patients.
My referral to a dermatologist was denied because the appointment was made before the referral was fully processed. By the time the referral came through the slot was gone.
I need mental health treatment urgently and cannot find a therapist through my insurance network. I ended up paying out of pocket because a 4-month wait isn't survivable.
The hospital for my emergency surgery was in-network. The surgeon who performed the surgery wasn't. I didn't pick my surgeon in an emergency. Now I owe $8,000 out-of-network.
My OB-GYN is in-network but the hospital she's affiliated with isn't for my plan. I either pay out-of-network rates or find a new OB at 5 months pregnant.
I'm trying to find an in-network sleep specialist. Your directory shows 4 in my area. Two have retired, one doesn't take insurance, and one isn't accepting new patients.
Telemedicine appointment was supposed to be covered at the same rate as an in-person primary care visit. It was billed at the specialist visit rate which is significantly higher.
I was referred to a specialist who is in-network but the testing facility they use is out-of-network. Nobody warns you about this arrangement when you schedule the appointment.
After my surgery I was in a rehabilitation facility for 10 days. My insurance only covered 5 days. The remaining 5 days cost me $14,000. Nobody explained this limitation before surgery.
My plan has a mental health visit limit of 30 sessions per year but my therapist says my condition requires more. My attorney says this may violate mental health parity laws.
Telehealth options are great in theory but none of the telehealth providers my insurance covers are able to prescribe controlled substances which is what I need for my ADHD.
I was on a waitlist at an in-network hospital for a scheduled procedure. They took me off the waitlist without notifying me because the slot expired. Now I'm starting the process over.
My gastroenterologist is 2 hours away because no in-network GI doctors are closer to me. For a quarterly chronic condition appointment this is not a sustainable situation.
My insurance plan lists mental health benefits but finding a covered provider who is culturally competent for my background and community is nearly impossible.
Went to an in-network urgent care. They referred me to an adjacent ER for additional testing. The ER was out-of-network and cost me $3,200 I wasn't expecting.
I tried to use telemedicine for a dermatology consult. The provider said I'd need to be seen in person anyway. Charged me a full specialist copay for a 5-minute video call.
My autism spectrum diagnosis requires an extensive evaluation. The in-network provider who does these evaluations has a 2-year waiting list in my state.
Provider directory showed my physical therapist as in-network. She wasn't. Cost me $1,800 in unexpected out-of-network charges. The directory is dangerously inaccurate.
I need a second opinion on a cancer diagnosis. The hospital I want for the second opinion isn't in my network. Getting out-of-network approval is a 30-day process.
My son broke his arm on a Saturday. The urgent care said he needed orthopedic surgery. The on-call orthopedic surgeon at the in-network hospital was not in-network. We had no choice.
My telehealth mental health appointment was cancelled 3 hours before by the provider with no explanation and no immediate reschedule option. Just a message to call back later.
Your app kicked me out in the middle of entering my payment information. When I logged back in the payment wasn't saved and I had to start the entire process over again.
I've been trying to find my member ID card on the app for a week. Every time I navigate to the ID card section the screen goes completely blank.
The portal shows my deductible as $0 remaining but when I called the amount is different. Which number is right? I need this information to make a real financial decision.
I cannot log into the member portal. I reset my password, got the reset email, set a new password and then it says the password is wrong. I've done this cycle 4 times now.
Your website is not mobile-friendly at all. I have to pinch and scroll constantly to see basic information. It is 2024 and this is still a significant problem.
I can't find where to submit a prior authorization request online. The portal supposedly has this feature but I cannot locate it anywhere. Everything just directs me to call instead.
My claims history only shows the last 6 months. I need to see claims from last year for my tax returns. There's no way to change the date range in the portal.
EOB documents take 3 to 5 minutes to load if they load at all. Most of the time I just get an error message after waiting.
The app doesn't recognize my face ID anymore even though I set it up and it worked perfectly for months. Now I have to type my password every single time.
I tried to update my primary care physician on the portal and got an error message every time I submitted. Had to call and wait on hold for 40 minutes to do a simple update.
My copay information shows two different amounts in two different places on the same portal. Which one is the accurate number I should expect to pay?
The provider directory search on your website only lets me search by name. I should be able to filter by specialty, distance, and whether they're accepting new patients.
I tried to send a secure message through the portal about my claim. It said the message was sent. Never got a reply. 3 weeks later I still have no idea if it went through.
Your app crashes every time I try to download an EOB document. I need these documents for a medical expense reimbursement account submission.
The portal shows I have a $500 credit from an overpayment but there's no way to request a refund online. The only option is to call. Why can't I handle this digitally?
I had to create a new account because I couldn't remember my username. The system won't let me create a new account because my email is already registered. I'm completely locked out.
My coverage information says it was updated today but the effective dates shown are from 3 months ago. The page is displaying stale and potentially incorrect information.
I found an error in my personal information on the portal. The update button doesn't work at all. Spent 20 minutes trying to submit a simple address change unsuccessfully.
The chat bot on your website is completely useless. It can't answer any specific questions and just tells me to call. Why offer a chat option that can't help with anything?
My digital ID card shows an incorrect group number. When I try to use it at a provider they get an error. I've been carrying a handwritten correction note.
I submitted a claim reimbursement request through the portal and received no confirmation it was received. No email, no confirmation number, nothing in my portal history at all.
My prescription drug information on the portal is showing medications I haven't taken in 3 years. The medications I actually take currently aren't listed anywhere.
The benefit summary PDF on your portal is from last year. It is March and I still cannot see my current year plan documents anywhere on the portal.
Every time I check the portal my deductible amount changes. It went from $1,200 to $1,400 to $1,150 over 3 days with no claims activity. I need to know the real number.
Two-factor authentication sends a code to my old phone number. I can't change the phone number without first receiving a code at the old number. I am completely locked out.
I've been trying to find the claim appeal form on the portal for 2 days. A help article says it's under claims but it simply isn't there.
The portal shows my annual physical as pending but I had it 4 months ago and the provider confirmed the claim was submitted. The status hasn't updated in months.
The cost estimator tool gave me a quote of $250 for a procedure that ended up costing $1,400. The estimate was completely wrong and I was not financially prepared for the difference.
I can view my EOBs but I cannot print them. The print function doesn't work in either Chrome or Edge. I need physical copies for a legal matter and have no way to get them.
I enrolled in paperless statements but still receive paper mail. Then my email notifications stopped. Now I'm getting neither digital nor paper EOBs reliably.
I downloaded the app when my plan renewed. It's been 3 months and the app still displays my old plan information and benefits from last year.
The portal logged me out automatically while I was filling out a claim form. All my work was lost. There is no auto-save feature and no warning before the session expires.
I searched for appeal in the portal help search and got zero results. The appeals process isn't documented anywhere accessible in the member portal.
My account shows family coverage but only my information appears when I log in. I can't see my spouse's claims or EOBs despite having an active family plan.
The mobile app shows a notification badge but when I open it there's nothing new to see. This has been happening for weeks and I'm worried I'm missing important information.
I tried using the portal cost comparison tool to find pricing for a surgery. It returned no results for my zip code. The tool doesn't appear to work for rural areas at all.
I can't find my drug formulary anywhere in the member portal. The link from the help page goes to a 404 error page.
The portal shows my out-of-pocket maximum as $6,000 individual but my plan documents say $4,500. These two official sources should match. I don't know which one is right.
I set up autopay through the portal and my premium was still charged manually. When I look at payment settings autopay shows as active. Then why was I charged manually?
My explanation of benefits emails link me to the portal but the link in the email doesn't work. I get a session error and have to log in separately every single time.
I tried to add my newborn to my plan through the portal within the 30-day qualifying event window. The portal said the change was submitted. Two months later she still isn't on my plan.
The portal health tracker feature asks me to connect my fitness tracker but the connection always fails. Based on the support forum this has been broken for 6 months.
I need to download all my EOBs for the year for a legal matter. There's no bulk download option. I have to download 47 individual documents one at a time.
The provider search shows a distance filter but it always defaults back to 50 miles no matter what custom distance I set. The setting doesn't stick.
I updated my email address on the portal 3 separate times over 2 months. Statements and EOBs are still going to my old email address. The update simply isn't saving.
The member portal and the mobile app show different information for the same claims. I don't know which source I'm supposed to trust for accurate information.
I used the find a doctor tool and carefully selected all my filters. Then I hit search and the page refreshed and lost all my filter selections. I had to start over 5 times.
My wellness program points are not showing up in the portal even though I completed the required activities weeks ago. I should be receiving a premium discount for completing them.
The portal session times out after 5 minutes of inactivity. Trying to fill out any longer form means constantly fighting the automatic timeout.
I can see a claim is denied in the portal but there's no reason given and no option to appeal directly from the portal view. I have to call just to find out why it was denied.
